title: Persist procedure/task failure telemetry on direct CLI termination

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implementing direct CLI failure telemetry for procedure runs. Scope: catch SIGINT/SIGTERM, uncaught exceptions, click.Abort, and non-zero SystemExit; persist FAILED status and structured failure details into Procedure metadata + Task error fields; fail current/pending TaskStages; fix chat session error branches that currently end as COMPLETED. No heartbeat/watcher/reconciler and no schema change.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented direct CLI procedure failure telemetry without schema changes. Added a shared failure path in `plexus/cli/shared/experiment_runner.py` that: installs SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers after task creation, persists `Procedure.status=RUNNING` plus `metadata.runtime` at start, and routes failed result / uncaught exception / click.Abort / non-zero SystemExit / signal termination through one idempotent finalizer that writes `Procedure.status=FAILED`, `metadata.last_failure`, Task `errorMessage/errorDetails`, and fails remaining TaskStages. Updated Tactus storage adapter to preserve unrelated Procedure.metadata keys during state/checkpoint saves so runtime/failure telemetry is not erased. Fixed Lua DSL runtime error branches to end chat sessions as FAILED instead of COMPLETED. Validation: `pytest -q plexus/cli/shared/test_experiment_runner.py plexus/cli/procedure/test_storage_metadata_preservation.py plexus/cli/procedure/lua_dsl/test_runtime_failure_session_status.py` => 8 passed. Also passed `python -m py_compile` on modified source/test files.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Follow-up safety hardening: adjusted the new Procedure metadata merge helper so a transient metadata-read failure does not write a partial metadata envelope back to the record. It now degrades to status-only update instead of risking checkpoint/state loss. Re-ran targeted validation after the change: 8 tests passed and py_compile remained clean.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting live termination integration test. Stopping existing optimizer process PID 97825 so the test run is isolated, then launching a fresh direct CLI procedure run and planning to SIGTERM it after ~2 minutes to verify FAILED telemetry persists to Procedure/Task/TaskStage records.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Live integration test passed. Launched direct CLI optimizer run, let it reach execution, then sent SIGTERM after ~2 minutes. Verified persisted backend state for procedure 00e8613d-da5b-4374-8441-b0696f4b601a / task 9a30ae38-52e5-4283-9e66-43f54b92a56a: Procedure.status=FAILED; Procedure.metadata.last_failure={kind: signal, signal: SIGTERM, phase: Evaluation, pid: 41508, terminated_at: 2026-04-20T17:03:16.542515+00:00}; Task.status=FAILED; Task.errorMessage='Procedure run interrupted by SIGTERM'; Task.errorDetails carries the same structured payload; Task stages flipped from stale running state to FAILED with visible statusMessage 'Procedure run interrupted by SIGTERM'. This demonstrates the feature working against real GraphQL data, not just unit tests.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Transitioning from kill-path validation to a real long-run integration test. Next step: start a fresh direct CLI optimizer run targeting 10 cycles and monitor startup/execution to confirm it gets beyond the initial cycle instead of dying immediately.
